tau_definition.summary: The extended lemniscate is the τ-categorical pushout of two SelfDesc agents' individual lemniscates along a shared signal channel S in their exterior lobes: L_ext(A,B) = L_A ∪_S L_B. It is the formal carrier of communication — the topological space across which one evaluator's signals reach another's wedge point — and the substrate on which language and social structure live.
tau_definition.categorical_invariant: Topological pushout of two figure-eight lemniscates along a sub-arc of their exterior lobes (VI.D70); fundamental group π₁(L_ext) is the amalgamated free product of π₁(L_A) ≅ ℤ * ℤ and π₁(L_B) ≅ ℤ * ℤ over the shared channel.

tau_definition.summary: Generation time is the τ-categorical period of the carrier-lineage's reproduction loop on τ¹: the average time between corresponding events in successive generations of a finite-lineage carrier. It is the temporal step of the PPAS prover (natural selection) on the fitness landscape, and the unit in which polynomial-time evolutionary convergence is counted.
tau_definition.categorical_invariant: Period T_gen of the lineage-level α-winding (lift of the cell cycle to the multicellular consumer carrier), and the discrete time-step of the PPAS verification round on the fitness landscape (VI.D50, VI.T27).

empirical_correlate.biomarker: Time from birth to median first reproduction across taxa; molecular-clock substitution rate per generation; demographic generation time T = ∑ x · l(x) · m(x) / ∑ l(x) · m(x) (Lotka equation), where l(x) is survivorship and m(x) is age-specific fecundity.
empirical_correlate.measurable_range: E. coli T_gen ≈ 20 min (optimal); yeast T_gen ≈ 90 min; Drosophila T_gen ≈ 2 weeks; mouse T_gen ≈ 10 weeks; human T_gen ≈ 25–30 yr; tortoise/whale T_gen ≈ 20–30 yr; lifetime offspring count covaries inversely (r/K life-history axis).
empirical_correlate.observation_method: Demographic life-table analysis, microbial doubling-time assays, longitudinal pedigree records, molecular-clock calibration against fossil record, age-structured population modelling.
